title: Listening - Part 3: A New Home
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about what they like about their new home. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main advantage each speaker describes. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about what they like about their new home. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main advantage each speaker describes. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about moving to a new home.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I used to rent a modern studio flat in the city centre. It was nice being near cafés and cinemas, but nearly all my salary disappeared on rent every month. I moved to an older flat slightly further out. [19]The most important difference is that the monthly cost is much lower, so I can finally put some money aside instead of worrying about every bill.[/19] I have to take a bus into town now, but that seems a small price to pay.||Speaker 2 Man: Our last house was on a busy road near traffic lights. Lorries passed early in the morning, and even at night we could hear cars stopping and starting outside the bedroom. The new place isn’t much bigger. [20]What I appreciate is being at the end of a little street where hardly any vehicles pass, so for the first time in years I can sleep with the window open.[/20] I hadn’t realised how much noise had affected me.||Speaker 3 Woman: When our baby was born, my partner and I lived two hours from both sets of grandparents. We managed, but visits needed careful planning and we rarely got a break. [21]We chose our new house because my parents now live just ten minutes away, which means they can drop in easily and help when we need them.[/21] The local shops aren’t great and I miss my old friends, but having family nearby has made everyday life far easier.||Speaker 4 Man: I restore old bicycles in my free time. In my previous flat, the kitchen floor was the only place I could work, which annoyed everyone and meant packing tools away constantly. Our new house has a small garage. [22]At last I can leave a bike on a stand, keep my tools organised and work on a repair whenever I have an hour free.[/22] My family is delighted that there are no longer bicycle parts next to the dining table.||Speaker 5 Woman: I was worried about moving because I’d spent years knowing everyone in my old apartment building. On our first day here, a woman next door brought us cake and gave us the recycling timetable. [23]Since then, people in the street have introduced themselves, offered tools and even invited us to a weekend barbecue, which made us feel welcome almost immediately.[/23] The house still needs decorating, but I’m sure choosing this neighbourhood was a very good decision.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I used to rent a modern studio flat in the city centre. It was nice being near cafés and cinemas, but nearly all my salary disappeared on rent every month. I moved to an older flat slightly further out. The most important difference is that the monthly cost is much lower, so I can finally put some money aside instead of worrying about every bill. I have to take a bus into town now, but that seems a small price to pay.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: Our last house was on a busy road near traffic lights. Lorries passed early in the morning, and even at night we could hear cars stopping and starting outside the bedroom. The new place isn’t much bigger. What I appreciate is being at the end of a little street where hardly any vehicles pass, so for the first time in years I can sleep with the window open. I hadn’t realised how much noise had affected me.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: When our baby was born, my partner and I lived two hours from both sets of grandparents. We managed, but visits needed careful planning and we rarely got a break. We chose our new house because my parents now live just ten minutes away, which means they can drop in easily and help when we need them. The local shops aren’t great and I miss my old friends, but having family nearby has made everyday life far easier.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I restore old bicycles in my free time. In my previous flat, the kitchen floor was the only place I could work, which annoyed everyone and meant packing tools away constantly. Our new house has a small garage. At last I can leave a bike on a stand, keep my tools organised and work on a repair whenever I have an hour free. My family is delighted that there are no longer bicycle parts next to the dining table.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I was worried about moving because I’d spent years knowing everyone in my old apartment building. On our first day here, a woman next door brought us cake and gave us the recycling timetable. Since then, people in the street have introduced themselves, offered tools and even invited us to a weekend barbecue, which made us feel welcome almost immediately. The house still needs decorating, but I’m sure choosing this neighbourhood was a very good decision.

options.text: There is space for their hobby.
Their journey to work is shorter.
There is an attractive outdoor area.
They live close to family members.
It costs less to live there.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker values the reduction in rent and resulting ability to save money (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker particularly values the peaceful location of the new home (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The new house was chosen so that the speaker's parents would live nearby and be able to help (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The garage gives the speaker a suitable permanent space for repairing bicycles (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker is especially pleased by the welcoming behaviour of nearby residents (evidence [23]).
